last_completed_item: D-140 — Shallow angled top-down viewpoint on a flat 2D gameplay plane
current_item: D-141 — Primary world conflict and enemy-faction scope
next_action: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting whether the game's main opposition is primarily a rogue-machine or automated industrial force, a biological or alien infestation, or a broader multi-faction science-fiction conflict whose one-level MVP focuses tightly on a single faction. Preserve the stylized action-science-fiction tone, mostly silent protagonist, menu-based hub, handcrafted missions with controlled variation, campaign-first replay, one-level internal vertical slice, fully 2D runtime, shallow angled top-down presentation, and the user's request to remain at the higher product and MVP level. Include enemy visual readability, weapon and loot fantasy, narrative flexibility, roster variety, asset reuse, boss potential, faction identity, future campaign expansion, and the danger of over-scoping the first level. Put the recommendation after all A/B/C options, at the end of the card. Queue accepted decisions D-141 through D-150 and persist the complete batch at D-150 unless a context handoff or explicit user instruction requires earlier persistence.
